response: Phone begins with the total number of games played, which is twelve. Phone calculates the number of games won and lost by using the relationship between wins and losses. Phone determines that the team won six games and lost three games. Phone uses the average points scored in wins to find the total points from victories. Phone multiplies the number of wins by the average points per win. Phone finds that the team scored one hundred sixty-eight points from wins. Phone uses the average points scored in losses to find the total points from defeats. Phone multiplies the number of losses by the average points per loss. Phone finds that the team scored forty-two points from losses. Phone adds the points from wins and losses to determine the total points scored. Phone calculates that the team scored two hundred ten points in total that season. Phone ensures that all words are unique and that the first word of each sentence is the word phone.
